description: Curated Traits & Personality SNP panel for Yeliztli (P3-62). PRS-primary module with weight sets for educational attainment (Okbay et al. 2022) and cognitive ability (Savage et al. 2018). Individual SNPs for neuroticism (CRHR1 rs242949), openness (RASA1-region rs1477268), conscientiousness (KATNAL2 rs2576037), and risk tolerance (CADM2 rs993137). DRD4 rs747302 proxy for dopamine receptor variation with coverage caveat. All findings hard-capped at ★★☆☆ (Moderate Evidence). Associative language only — no directive claims.
module_disclaimer: Traits & Personality findings reflect statistical associations observed in large population studies. They do not predict individual outcomes. Behavioral traits are shaped by complex interactions between many genes, environment, culture, and personal experience. These results are for research and educational purposes only.

pathways.name: Cognitive Ability & Educational Attainment
pathways.description: Polygenic risk scores derived from large-scale GWAS meta-analyses of cognitive performance and years of education. These scores reflect statistical associations at the population level and do not predict individual intelligence or academic achievement.

pathways.name: Personality Dimensions (Big Five)
pathways.description: Individual SNPs associated with selected Big Five personality dimensions from trait-matched GWAS. The curated single-SNP rows cover neuroticism, openness, and conscientiousness only; extraversion and agreeableness currently have no supported single-SNP rows. GWAS effect sizes for personality traits are very small, and these variants collectively explain only a tiny fraction of personality variation.

GG.effect_summary: No modeled T effect allele at the CRHR1 neuroticism-associated locus.

GT.effect_summary: One copy of the T allele at rs242949. This CRHR1-region locus is associated with neuroticism measurement in GWAS Catalog/Nagel et al. 2018; the cataloged direction is lower neuroticism z-score, so do not interpret it as higher neuroticism.

TG.effect_summary: One copy of the T allele at rs242949. This CRHR1-region locus is associated with neuroticism measurement in GWAS Catalog/Nagel et al. 2018; the cataloged direction is lower neuroticism z-score, so do not interpret it as higher neuroticism.

TT.effect_summary: Two copies of the T allele at rs242949. This CRHR1-region locus is associated with neuroticism measurement in GWAS Catalog/Nagel et al. 2018; the cataloged direction is lower neuroticism z-score. Individual SNP effects on personality are very small and non-deterministic.

snps.recommendation_text: Nagel et al. 2018 is a neuroticism-only GWAS meta-analysis (N>449,000). GWAS Catalog records rs242949 in the CRHR1 region for neuroticism measurement; individual SNP effects are extremely small and should not be interpreted as deterministic. Neuroticism is a normal personality dimension influenced by many genetic and environmental factors.

TT.effect_summary: No C effect allele at the 5q14.3 RASA1-region openness locus.

TC.effect_summary: One copy of the C allele. de Moor et al. 2012 reported this allele at rs1477268 as associated with higher openness to experience in discovery samples; the locus did not replicate significantly, and the effect is very small.

CT.effect_summary: One copy of the C allele. de Moor et al. 2012 reported this allele at rs1477268 as associated with higher openness to experience in discovery samples; the locus did not replicate significantly, and the effect is very small.

CC.effect_summary: Two copies of the C allele. de Moor et al. 2012 reported this allele at rs1477268 as associated with higher openness to experience in discovery samples; the locus did not replicate significantly, and the effect is very small.

snps.recommendation_text: de Moor et al. 2012 reported genome-wide-significant discovery-stage association for openness near RASA1 on 5q14.3 (rs1477268/rs2032794), but in silico replication was not significant. Treat this as research-interest only.

CC.effect_summary: No T effect allele at the KATNAL2 conscientiousness locus.

CT.effect_summary: One copy of the T allele. de Moor et al. 2012 reported this allele at rs2576037 as associated with lower conscientiousness in discovery samples; replication direction was consistent but not significant, and the effect is very small.

TC.effect_summary: One copy of the T allele. de Moor et al. 2012 reported this allele at rs2576037 as associated with lower conscientiousness in discovery samples; replication direction was consistent but not significant, and the effect is very small.

TT.effect_summary: Two copies of the T allele. de Moor et al. 2012 reported this allele at rs2576037 as associated with lower conscientiousness in discovery samples; replication direction was consistent but not significant, and the effect is very small.

snps.recommendation_text: de Moor et al. 2012 reported genome-wide-significant discovery-stage association for conscientiousness in KATNAL2 (rs2576037), with non-significant in silico replication but consistent effect direction. Treat this as research-interest only.

pathways.name: Behavioral Traits
pathways.description: Individual SNPs associated with behavioral phenotypes including risk tolerance and dopamine signaling. These associations are based on population-level GWAS data and do not predict individual behavior.

TT.effect_summary: No risk allele at CADM2 risk tolerance locus. No association with altered risk-taking behavior at this variant.

TC.effect_summary: One copy of the C allele. Associated with modestly higher self-reported willingness to take risks in GWAS of >1 million participants.

CT.effect_summary: One copy of the C allele. Associated with modestly higher self-reported willingness to take risks in GWAS of >1 million participants.

CC.effect_summary: Two copies of the C allele. Associated with modestly higher self-reported risk tolerance. Effect size is very small and not predictive of individual behavior.

snps.recommendation_text: CADM2 variants are among the most replicated risk tolerance GWAS hits (Karlsson Linnér et al. 2019, N>1 million). Risk tolerance is influenced by many genetic and environmental factors. This variant explains a tiny fraction of variation.

CC.effect_summary: No G allele at the DRD4 -616 C/G promoter SNP. No low-confidence rs747302 proxy signal is reported for the DRD4 exon III VNTR.

CG.effect_summary: One G allele at the DRD4 -616 C/G promoter SNP. This is only a low-confidence contextual proxy for the DRD4 exon III VNTR; direct VNTR status is not determined.

GC.effect_summary: One G allele at the DRD4 -616 C/G promoter SNP. This is only a low-confidence contextual proxy for the DRD4 exon III VNTR; direct VNTR status is not determined.

GG.effect_summary: Two G alleles at the DRD4 -616 C/G promoter SNP. This remains a low-confidence proxy context for the DRD4 exon III VNTR, not direct VNTR genotyping.

snps.recommendation_text: DRD4 exon III VNTR has been associated with novelty seeking and ADHD susceptibility in candidate gene studies, but effect sizes are small and replication is inconsistent. rs747302 is the DRD4 -616 C/G promoter SNP and is not direct VNTR genotyping; treat any signal as low-confidence context only.
snps.coverage_note: rs747302 is the DRD4 -616 C/G promoter SNP used here only as low-confidence proxy context for the exon III VNTR (7-repeat allele). Direct VNTR genotyping is not available on 23andMe/microarray platforms, and the SNP-to-VNTR mapping is not reliable enough for a confident VNTR call. Results should be interpreted with caution.

cross_module.note: DRD4 VNTR has been associated with ADHD susceptibility. See Gene Health module for ADHD-related findings.

prs_weight_sets.name: Educational attainment (Okbay 2022)
prs_weight_sets.calibration_note: Placeholder reference distribution (mean=0/std=1) is marked uncalibrated for this exact traits PRS subset and ancestry. The engine therefore withholds the population percentile / z-score / bootstrap CI for this score (issue #21). Supply a calibrated or analytically-derived reference_mean/reference_std and set calibrated=true to re-enable the percentile.

prs_weight_sets.name: Cognitive ability (Savage 2018)
prs_weight_sets.calibration_note: Placeholder reference distribution (mean=0/std=1) is marked uncalibrated for this exact traits PRS subset and ancestry. The engine therefore withholds the population percentile / z-score / bootstrap CI for this score (issue #21). Supply a calibrated or analytically-derived reference_mean/reference_std and set calibrated=true to re-enable the percentile.

scoring_rules.evidence_cap_description: All Traits & Personality findings are hard-capped at ★★☆☆ (Moderate Evidence) per PRD §3.4b, regardless of the underlying evidence level of individual SNPs or PRS components.
scoring_rules.prs_primary_description: This module uses PRS as the primary analysis method, reusing the generic PRS engine (P3-14). Individual SNPs are secondary and provide trait-level context.
scoring_rules.research_use_only_description: All PRS outputs must include 'Research Use Only' labels. PRS findings are never displayed alongside monogenic ClinVar findings.
scoring_rules.associative_language_rule: All finding text must use associative language ('associated with', 'linked to') rather than directive claims ('causes', 'determines', 'predicts'). No deterministic phrasing.
scoring_rules.drd4_proxy_rule: DRD4 rs747302 is the -616 C/G promoter SNP used only as low-confidence proxy context for the exon III VNTR. Results must display a coverage caveat noting that direct VNTR status is not determined.

DRD4_proxy.r_squared_note: No stable cross-ancestry LD value is encoded; published DRD4 VNTR evidence should not be treated as direct evidence for rs747302 genotype effects.
DRD4_proxy.insufficient_data_reason: Tag SNP proxy for structural variant (VNTR). Accuracy limited by LD decay and population-specific haplotype structure.

prs_ancestry_mismatch.description: Both PRS weight sets are derived from EUR populations. Ancestry mismatch warning is mandatory when user's inferred ancestry differs from EUR.

cross_module_links.note: DRD4 VNTR has been associated with ADHD susceptibility in candidate gene studies.
